titleStmt.title: Letter from Charles Scott to Thomas G. Buchanan, 14 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Charles Edward Scott

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Charles Edward Scott (1862-1940), rector of Douglas, Co. Cork, to Thomas G. Buchanan regarding the removal of Buchanan's relatives from the local cemetery to another burial plot.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Douglas Rectory. Cork. June 14. 1916. Dear Mr. Buchanan, The select vestry of Douglas
Parish who own the Cemetary are quite
willing that you should remove the
remains of members of yourfamily
from out Burial Plot to another
provided that you obtain the necessary
permission from the legal authorities.
I trust this statement in writing is
sufficient. In case any forms have to
he filled up please let us know & I shall
be glad to attend to them. Please excuse
my delay in answering your letter. One
of my chamber wardens is away & it takes
some days to get an answer from him. Yours sincerely C.E. Scott Rector of Douglas
